steps.voice: This is the Wi-Fi Settings screen — the full multi-network manager. The first-boot wizard has a simplified version of this same screen, but everything here is available any time you need it.
Status at the top-right shows what mode you're in and where you're connected. Green means connected, amber means scanning, red means something's wrong.

The Controls card on the left. Mode decides what role the dash plays — Off, WiFi Client, or Hotspot. Client mode joins an existing Wi-Fi network; Hotspot mode lets your phone or laptop connect directly to the dash itself.
Start on Boot decides what mode to come up in next power-on. Set it to Client if you're always at home, or Hotspot if you're out at a track.
Hotspot Password lets you set a WPA2 key for the dash's own network. Minimum eight characters. Tap Set to save.
Available Networks on the right. Tap Scan to refresh the list, then tap any network to connect. Lock icon means password-protected. Signal strength on the right tells you if it's worth joining.
Once you're connected, pop back to Device Settings — Network section shows your IP address. That's the URL you type into any laptop or phone to open the web editor.

For out-at-the-track situations where there's no shop Wi-Fi, switch Mode to Hotspot. The dash broadcasts its own network, you join from your phone, and you're editing the dash over the web UI in seconds.
Tap Back to return to Device Settings. Your Wi-Fi choice persists in NVS, so the next power-up rejoins automatically.
